item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This needs an interview

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neat to see what a simple rewrite can do for a piece, would actually love to see this one fleshed out a bit more. An interview would be interesting

item.title: No interview?
item.content:encoded: I think an interview would of been nice in order see more of an insight into its mind

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• lives in the Black Forest, Germany
  • loves nature
  • kills people
  • can't speak

"About ███ years after being taken into Foundation custody, the same village began receiving more missing persons reports. The cause of these was determined to be a damaged SST Laboratories Siege Automaton (designated E54), which was disabled and captured by Foundation personnel without further incident. Multiple behavioral similarities were found between SCP-1063 and this Bastion unit, but no anomalous properties have been found. Further testing is ongoing."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God dammit. Fucking Nazis ruining everything :(

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I could upvote comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although this blade is made of wood, it is razor-sharp, and is almost metallic in its mechanical properties; when in a rage state, SCP-1063 is able to sever human heads or limbs in a single blow, and to chop through over 30 (thirty) cm of granite, with no damage to the blade.

razor-sharp

Extremely sharp is more appropriate, but that quibble pales in comparison to

when in a rage state,

No SCP has ever made the term 'rage state' work. 'Rage state' is meaningless drivel that sounds cool. It's the kind of nonsense that one would hear Arthur Radiantgonads Rothschild-Pendragon say about a boss monster in Final Fantasy.

"Oh no! The Frumpious Cootchsnatcher has entered its rage state!'
And 3 years later this scip reaches 100 upvotes, congratulations!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of "The Sound of His Horn", an excellent alt-history short story that highlights the pastoral emphasis of Nazi ideology.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was the Eastern front. Schwarzwald was on the Western front.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The second purpose, which it claims to have been taught by "those nice soldiers who gave me the [swastika] armband", is to protect Germany from English-speaking invaders.

What about Russian?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always thought this was solid. Nothing exceptional, but a good read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not only is it an SCP it is also a bigot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He may not be "scarily competent".

He may not be very smart, or very any good at fighting.

But he's still got a fucking huge Murder Axe for a hand, and he will try to kill you with it if he thinks you deserve it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. A little comical relief now and then never hurt anyone. I hadn't originally intended this character to be 'buffoonish', but this is a great improvement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nazis are apparently to SCPs what water is to jello.

Instant awesome, just add Nazis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how buffoonish this guy is. It's a nice contrast to all those scarily competent SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoa— pulled from -21 into the positives. Friggin' impressive, Voct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Hello, mister SCP with the murder-axe for a hand! I notice that you have poor skills in unarmed combat! Would you like us to teach you?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There used to be a top hat, a long long time ago. It's gone now, but the hatband remains.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something I'm a bit confused about: is he wearing a top-hat like before, or is the hat band just sorta hanging out on top of his head?

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Well… its at -4… Cancel my deletion vote?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks Voct for saving this. I owe you one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. He's definitely got an advantage in a one-on-one fight but is easily subdued by a group.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or something like this: "Foundation personnel surveyed the region while disguised as a search party, and were attacked by SCP-1063 when they began to to prepare a campfire. The Subject was quickly subdued with no casualties."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe something to the effect of: "His low intelligence is reflected in his lack of basic combat skills and inability/disinterest in learning any. It is presumed that his prior attacks were successful due to the element of surprise and luck."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haven't figured out how yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it would help if you added a line to that effect in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the record, he's not a good-fightin' robot man. He fights fuckin' LOUSY.

The only reason he was able to kill so many people is a) the element of surprise, b) he attacked them one by one, and c) he's got a fuckin' huge murder-axe for a hand.

When he went up against a team of Foundation agents…. well, a) they're trained in working together, b) they were expecting something anomalous, and c) they know karate.

They took him down in three minutes, no casualties.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who says the King who ennobled him was human?

And it's true that "Germany" as a cohesive didn't exist back in the 19th century when this guy was created. It did exist when the Nazis found him in the '40s.

As for the brass vs wood, that was a holdover from the first version, and I'll change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eco-Nazi is an improvement, but it's not entirely there.

It doesn't make sense that he would want to protect "Germany", given that the concept of Germany as a political entity didn't exist before Kaiser Bill. Given that this guy's habitat was in the Black Forest, the best candidate for the king in question is Frederick I of Württemberg, or possibly his son William I (Württemberg was the only kingdom in the Black Forest area in the 19th century.

Also, some of the prose could be improved: "SCP-1063 is a humanoid automaton which appears to be constructed almost entirely out of wood, with highly articulated joints made of brass ball bearings of various sizes. " The entire joint wouldn't be made of ball bearings; and it kind of contradicts where you just were with "almost entirely out of wood". Wood bearings do exist; why not just make him entirely out of wood?

I like the reference to Fraktur calligraphy. It is indeed weird looking.

This is the kind of article that would be strongly improved with a photo. Why not find one somewhere (google images found this in 10 seconds: http://4.bp.blogspot.com/_SURKoZUIukM/RlIH7ktZiTI/AAAAAAAAAiY/aT6zZvpjPg0/s640/Statue+Wonderful+2.jpg which is not what you are going for but it demonstrates that there are images out there) and alter the description to match whatever photo you like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just re-read it… I still don't really like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't read it the first time through… and while I have no doubt that it's been improved in quality it's still a good fightin' wooden robot man. Even a well written logically consistent good fightin' wooden robot man gets a -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. Voct's rewrite has turned this around for me. -1 to +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After the rewrite, I now officially like this piece. Hopefully enough people will agree to reverse or counterbalance the -'s

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: I'll be doing that tomorrow morning.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Fourthing if it's not rewritten by that time.

item.title: Re: Notions
item.content:encoded: As do I.

item.title: Re: Notions
item.content:encoded: I approve.

item.title: Re: Notions
item.content:encoded: Hmmm… Okay, fair enough.

item.title: Re: Notions
item.content:encoded:
Changes to its appearance: well, it'll be wearing a tattered swastika armband. But aside from that, it still looks pretty much the way you described it.

How did the Nazis find it: does it really matter? I think it's important that it was found accidentally by a squad of soldiers, but as for the rest… use your imagination.

item.title: Re: Notions
item.content:encoded: Jesus. No wonder people commission you for rewrites. I absolutely love this and 100% percent for it. I like the idea of it having absolutely no understanding of the evil associated with the swastika and instead adoring it for it's original meaning and association with it's native Germany. I also like the bit about it calmly taking the potted plant away before attacking a person, almost adds a comical effect. Anyway, it's great! But, could I ask if there will be any changes to appearance? How did the Nazis find it? And will there be any additional documents of the thing's origin given by the thing itself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I suspect he has a few of them. Borrowed them from littering hikers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this guy just needs a heart.

item.title: Notions
item.content:encoded:
First, I'm gonna be re-doing this in proper clinical style - sorry, Stanton, you still don't quite have the hang of it.

Also, a rename: the villagers may have called it the Wooden Demon, you may have called it "the Woodsman", but it calls itself Freiherr von Schwarzwald: the Baron (or 'Free Lord') of the Black Forest. It sees itself as nobility. To be precise, it sees itself as German nobility. It is an extremely bad idea to speak English when you're within, oh, 15 meters of this thing.

It can't speak, but it can write (in beautiful ornate Fraktur). That's how we know it's not very smart.

Containment requires several live plants, as these encourage it to moderate its aggressive behavior. It won't attack you if you're holding a live potted plant (although it may gently take the plant away from you and then attack). If it ever gets really out of control, we're pretty sure it's flammable.

Oh, and it wears a swastika armband. A very old and tattered swastika armband, which it says was a gift. It had been 'asleep' for decades when the Nazis found it, and it was taught a very simplified version of their ideology - basically 'protect us against foreign invaders, especially the ones who speak English'. It has no real understanding of politics or ethnic issues, and does not react to the Star of David, the Stars and Stripes, or the Union Jack. It loves swastikas, though (clockwise and counterclockwise both), and says that they represent Germany and the nourishing light of the Sun.

It never met Hitler, but Hitler was told about it.

What do you think?

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks for suggesting a rewrite before the deletion police took care of it. I appreciate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Permission granted. God speed.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Concurring. He did good with the last one, so…

Vote on hold pending Voct's rewrite

You've got until… Lets say this coming Friday, February 3rd.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Stay of deletion request, pending Voct's rewrite?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
REQUEST PERMISSION TO REWRITE

but i'll need a few days

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Thirding.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Second.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Suggesting deletion at -15.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ditto. It was a cool living steampunk thing until "oh, and has a big tumor with an axe". Getting down to the hostility made it worse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate to admit this, but that was pretty good. Bravo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed. I've saved the kittens!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I wouldn't say major. But, I still fixed the bits that didn't sound clinical enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't worry a major rewrite is happening now

item.title: Re: UGH!
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I'm still getting used to the clinical style.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This can be easily saved by a rewrite.
